text: Computer vision is a field of artificial intelligence that enables computers to understand and process visual information from the world, just like humans do with their eyes. Image recognition is one of its core tasks, allowing computers to identify objects, people, or places in photos. For example, social media platforms use it to automatically tag friends in uploaded pictures. Another important area is object detection, where the computer not only identifies objects but also locates them within an image. This technology has many practical applications. In security, it helps surveillance systems detect unusual behaviour. In manufacturing, it checks products for defects. More impressively, self-driving cars rely heavily on computer vision to recognise traffic signs, pedestrians, and other vehicles, making safe driving decisions in real time.
